text: A union representing healthcare workers has demanded an immediate review of hospital cleanliness standards after a rat was spotted in a kitchen at a major hospital. The sighting has raised concerns about the potential for rodent-borne illnesses and the overall cleanliness of the facility. The union has called for a thorough investigation into the matter and has requested that the hospital take immediate action to address the issue. Hospital administration has acknowledged the incident and has promised to take all necessary measures to ensure the cleanliness and safety of the facility. Patients and staff have expressed concern about the situation, and some have called for increased measures to prevent rodent infestations in the future. The incident has highlighted the need for vigilance in maintaining hospital cleanliness and the importance of regular inspections and pest control measures. An investigation is currently underway to determine how the rat entered the hospital and to identify any potential health risks.

In a renewed effort to uncover the truth behind the mysterious disappearance of Madeleine McCann, authorities have announced that a significant phase of operational activity is set to commence in the coming weeks. The revelation comes as police continue to tirelessly pursue leads and gather evidence in one of the most high-profile missing persons cases of the past decade. With renewed determination, law enforcement officials are hopeful that this latest phase will bring them closer to finding answers and providing long-awaited closure to Madeleine's family. As the investigation intensifies, the world watches on, holding onto the hope that justice will ultimately prevail in this heartbreaking case.

**Assistant****Headline**: "False Alarm: Resin Causes Confusion at Newport Shopping Centre"**Body**:Several people at a Newport shopping centre were left feeling unwell due to a perceived gas smell.However, the South Wales Ambulance Service revealed that the odor was actually caused by resin used by workmen.The incident serves as a reminder of the importance of accurate information in emergency situations.Authorities urge the public to remain calm and rely on official sources for accurate information during similar events.Resin used in construction can sometimes emit strong odors, leading to confusion and unnecessary alarm.

**Abstract**This article presents the merits of utilizing variational geomagnetic data assimilation. It is an innovative method that leverages observations to enhance the precision of numerical models. The approach combines concepts from optimal estimation theory and the laws of physics governing the behavior of the magnetohydrodynamic (MHD) system, which describes the dynamics of electrically conducting fluids like Earth's liquid outer core.In this study, a simplified version of the MHD system is considered—a one-dimensional, nonlinear model with sparsely distributed observations. This setup is designed to mimic critical aspects of the geomagnetic field's behavior. We demonstrate the method's effectiveness in recovering the true state of the system from limited and noisy measurements. The findings underscore the utility of variational geomagnetic data assimilation, especially in scenarios with limited and noisy observations. The study highlights the benefits of this approach and paves the way for further exploration and implementation in more complex and realistic settings.

Trials for the event, between 30 June and 2 July, clash with the Games.The 400m runner, 20, shaved seven hundredths of a second off his island record on his way to winning the England Under-23 title on Sunday."He's now seeing that opportunity there for him," Guernsey team manager Tom Druce told BBC Sport."I think they've looked at the current landscape of running - some of the top guys aren't performing, quite frankly, so he's gone from a maybe to a probable [for the World Championships]."Chalmers, who won the British Universities and Colleges Sport 400m title in a record time last month, had hoped to win three gold medals at the Gotland Games.He will make his senior Great Britain debut at the European Team Championships this weekend, and has also been selected for the European Under-23 Championships in Poland next month.His place in Guernsey's 4x400m Island Games relay squad has been taken by Peter Curtis."Peter's one of our most promising Under-17s, and he would have been named in the team anyway if we didn't already have two high-class 400m hurdlers," added Druce.

In an old dusty attic hidden deep within the heart of a forgotten town, I stumbled upon a treasure trove of history. Among the cobwebs and boxes, I discovered a stack of weathered journals, their pages yellowed with age. Upon closer inspection, I realized that these were the journals of war survivors from a forgotten era.The first journal belonged to a soldier who fought valiantly in the trenches of World War I. His words painted a vivid picture of the horrors he witnessed, the deafening sounds of gunfire and the acrid stench of death. He wrote of comradery and the unwavering spirit that kept him going, despite the relentless onslaught.The second journal revealed the heartbreaking accounts of a nurse during the Vietnam War. She described the endless stream of wounded soldiers arriving at the field hospital, their bodies broken and spirits shattered. Her words captured both the despair and resilience of those who tended to the wounded, offering solace amidst chaos.Another journal chronicled the experiences of a child living through the ravages of World War II. Her innocent words were filled with confusion and fear, as she witnessed the destruction of her home and the loss of loved ones. Yet, amidst the darkness, her spirit remained unbroken, finding solace in small acts of kindness and hope for a brighter future.As I delved deeper into the journals, I encountered stories from soldiers who fought in various conflicts throughout history. Each entry was a testament to the human spirit, showcasing the unyielding determination to survive against all odds. They wrote of love and loss, of bravery and sacrifice, and of the scars that would forever etch their souls.The journals transported me to different times and places, immersing me in the harsh realities and profound depths of human experience during war. They spoke of the untold stories of those who had been forgotten by time, ensuring their memories would live on.

Funeral Costs Soar in Wales as Average Price Jumps by £170 in One YearNew data has revealed a concerning trend in funeral expenses in Wales, as the average cost has surged by £170 over the past year, reaching a staggering £3,461. These figures, which highlight the financial burden faced by grieving families, have sparked concerns over the accessibility and affordability of funerals. The rise in funeral costs, which is significantly higher than the overall inflation rate, adds pressure to families already grappling with the emotional and logistical challenges of arranging a loved one's final farewell.Experts suggest that various factors contribute to this sharp increase in funeral prices, including the rising costs of essential services, such as cremation or burial, professional fees, and the purchase of a coffin or casket. Additionally, the COVID-19 pandemic has also impacted the funeral industry, leading to increased demand for funeral services, leading to higher prices. Campaigners and advocacy groups are calling for increased transparency in funeral costs, urging the government and industry regulators to address the issue and provide support to grieving families who struggle to meet these financial burdens. The Welsh government is now under pressure to take action and explore options to mitigate the rising costs, ensuring that funerals remain accessible for all, regardless of income or socioeconomic status.With the cost of funerals continuing to rise, families are left grappling with difficult decisions, often resorting to cutting corners or accumulating debt to cover the expenses. The emotional toll combined with the financial strain amplifies the distress experienced during such already challenging times. For many, the soaring funeral costs have become an added hardship during the grieving process, emphasizing the urgent need for a comprehensive solution to alleviate the burden faced by Welsh families in their time of loss.

The Lionesses' third-place finish in Canada makes them the second most successful England team ever, only behind the men's victory in 1966.Sampson replaced former boss Hope Powell in August 2013."It's been instilled in us over the last 18 months, that family atmosphere, the togetherness," said White."It's 23 players, we're all in it. You can't win it without 23 players or the backroom staff as well."During Powell's 15-year reign as manager, the Lionesses never progressed beyond the World Cup quarter-finals - and former players have since criticised the negative feeling among the team during her time in charge.However, speaking to BBC Radio Three Counties, 26-year-old White says it has been a different story under Sampson."He's changed a lot in the team, he's brought in new people," said White. "I always think it's good to have a change."He's definitely brought that togetherness, that fire, that passion. Everyone was on board with everything."We all stuck together and we're definitely like a little family."

"*ZEEEEEEEETAAAAA! * "Yells echoed everywhere around me. Xera, the queen herself, was out looking for the girl, too. Well, I suppose that you' d assume she would look for her own daughter. Vengeance light up her eyes, and I knew that whoever had taken the princess would not get off very easily. Usually, when someone went missing, we would wait a day or two to see if they showed up. This time, the Guard was called immediately and a search was started. Someone had taken the princess, and that wasn' t a petty, forgettable crime. Ranging up and down the foothills, I could find no tracks of any kind. Quite literally, the kidnappers had left no trail. Possibly, they' d run off through the trees, but it seemed unlikely that they could do that carrying the 100 pound princess. Over every hill, there was nothing but the empty forest to greet me and taunt me. No signs of any disturbance. Moreover, the sky was beginning to darken, and the trail would go cold, soon. Lazily drifting, I plodded along the path until I found a tree that looked capable of supporting my weight and providing a comfortable night' s rest. Kicking off my shoes, I prepared to climb. Just as I placed my hands on the trunk, I noticed a shining piece of fabric caught in a branch, higher up. Impossible. Half excited, half scared, I climbed the tree. I observed the fabric closely. Gold griffins looked up at me, sewn into the fabric with an expert' s hand. Feeling it confirmed that it was silk, and upon a moment' s thought I remembered the design on the princess' s dress. Excited for the thrill of the chase, I took off to tell the King I' d found a clue. During my reckless flight, though, I tripped and crashed into the ground. Catching my breath slowly, I stood up. Blood was everywhere, and I knew none of it was mine. At my feet was the princess.

As I stood there, perplexed by the sight in front of me, my curiosity got the better of me. Three monkey statues, meticulously carved and placed on a pedestal, caught my attention. It was an odd encounter in the heart of an ancient temple. The note nearby had a cryptic message: "See no evil, hear no evil, speak no evil." I couldn't resist the urge to investigate further. Carefully, I analyzed each statue, their eyes covered, ears sealed, and mouths shut tight. The first statue had its hands over the eyes, symbolizing the act of "seeing no evil." Tentatively, I reached out and touched the smooth surface of its stone hand. Suddenly, a wave of warmth surged through my body, revealing a hidden passage behind the statues. I took a deep breath and moved on. The second statue had its ears covered, signifying the concept of "hearing no evil." I hesitated for a moment, then extended my hand to touch its ear. A faint sound echoed in the distance, growing louder with each passing second. The ground trembled beneath me as a secret door emerged from the floor, granting me access to the unknown. The last statue stood proudly, with its hands tightly pressed against its mouth, representing "speaking no evil." My heart raced as I reached out and touched its lips. Instantly, the air crackled with energy, and the space around me shifted, revealing a magnificent chamber filled with ancient artifacts and untold treasures. I marveled at the wonders in front of me, each item telling a story of its own. However, amidst the allure of treasure, a realization dawned upon me—it was not material wealth that was sought here, but the wisdom behind the ancient proverb. The monkey statues were guardians, testing the purity of my intentions. I understood that the real riches lie in the virtues of honesty, integrity, and respect. With newfound enlightenment, I retraced my steps to the entrance of the temple, leaving behind the wealth I had discovered.

In this paper we obtain general integral formulas for probabilities in theasymmetric simple exclusion process (ASEP) on the integer lattice with nearestneighbor hopping rates p to the right and q=1-p to the left. For the most partwe consider an N-particle system but for certain of these formulas we can takethe limit as N goes to infinity. First we obtain, for the N-particle system, aformula for the probability of a configuration at time t, given the initialconfiguration. For this we use Bethe Ansatz ideas to solve the master equation,extending a result of Schuetz for the case N=2. The main results of the paper,derived from this, are integral formulas for the probability, for given initialconfiguration, that the m'th left-most particle is at x at time t. In one ofthese formulas we can take the limit as N goes to infinity, and it gives theprobability for an infinite system where the initial configuration is boundedon one side. For the special case of the totally asymmetric simple exclusionprocess (TASEP) our formulas reduce to the known ones.

A cantankerous cockerel named "Gerry" has become the subject of controversy at a popular tourist attraction in England. The bird, known for its aggressive behavior, has been threatened with eviction from the estate where it resides in order to protect visitors from potential attacks. Despite its grumpy demeanor, Gerry has become a beloved fixture among tourists and locals alike, with many calling for its reprieve. The estate's management is currently weighing its options and seeking alternative solutions to ensure the safety of its guests while also allowing Gerry to remain on the premises.

The eggs of a mosquito capable of transmitting tropical diseases, including the Zika virus, have been found for the first time in the UK. The eggs were discovered in a small area of southeast England, and experts believe they may have been brought to the country through global trade or travel. The mosquito species, known as Aedes aegypti, is native to tropical regions and is known to carry a number of diseases, including Zika, dengue fever, and chikungunya. Public health officials are taking steps to monitor the situation and prevent the spread of the disease. They are urging people to take precautions against mosquito bites, such as using insect repellent and wearing protective clothing.

As I boarded the ship, I couldn't help but feel a sense of excitement and nervousness. I had just signed onto a freelance mercenary crew, and this was my first mission. I had no idea what to expect, but I was eager to prove myself.As I made my way to the briefing room, I saw a group of heavily armed and armored individuals gathered around a large table. My Squad Leader, a tall, imposing figure with a scar above his left eyebrow, stood at the front of the room."Alright, listen up," he said, his voice firm and commanding. "Our mission is to extract a high-value target from a hostile environment. We have intel that they are being held captive by a prominent crime lord in the city."He gestured to a holographic projection of the city that appeared on the table. "Our target is located in this building, in the middle of the city. We will need to be stealthy and quick, as the crime lord has a large security force at his disposal."I felt a sense of pride and responsibility wash over me as the SL handed me a custom-made rifle. "This is your weapon, tailored to your specifications. Use it wisely."As we prepared to deploy, I couldn't help but feel a sense of awe at the skills and expertise of my fellow crew members. They were all seasoned veterans, each with their own unique set of skills and abilities.We boarded a small dropship and set off towards our target. The ride was bumpy, but our pilot expertly navigated through the city's tight spaces.As we approached the building, the SL gave the signal, and we all fell silent, waiting for the go-ahead. Suddenly, the door burst open, and we stormed inside.The mission was a blur of gunfire and chaos, but we worked together seamlessly, each of us playing our part to perfection. I took out several guards, while the others secured the target and prepared for extraction.It wasn't long before we had the target safely in tow, and we made our way back to the dropship.

It was just an ordinary day, and I found myself at home, going about my usual routines. As I walked past the hallway mirror, something caught my eye, causing me to freeze in my tracks. I saw her—my sister, Sarah, who had tragically passed away years ago. At first, I thought it was a trick of my imagination or a mere reflection, but her presence was undeniably real. Her bright eyes sparkled with warmth as a gentle smile played upon her lips. The sight both thrilled and confused me, filling my heart with a mixture of joy and sorrow. With cautious steps, I approached the mirror, my hand trembling with anticipation. Ignoring my rational mind, I reached out, hoping to feel her warmth, to connect with her spirit once more. To my astonishment, my hand effortlessly passed through the glass, as if it were nothing more than a veil separating our worlds. A rush of emotions overwhelmed me, and tears welled up in my eyes. Though I couldn't hear her voice, I felt her presence, an ethereal connection that transcended the physical realm. Tentatively, I moved closer and rested my palm against her gently fading image. It was a bittersweet moment of reunion, a precious encounter that defied all logical explanation. Sarah's presence brought me solace, knowing that she was still watching over me. We spent what felt like an eternity gazing at each other, silently communicating a depth of love and longing. But as the seconds ticked by, her essence began to wane, gradually fading back into the depths of the mirror. Panic seized my heart as I desperately clung to her vanishing form, but it was futile. Slowly, the image dissolved, leaving behind a void of emptiness that echoed the loss I had felt for so long. With tear-streaked cheeks, I whispered my final words of love and farewell. Although parted once again, I carried an inextinguishable flame of hope within my soul. From that day forward, I silently vowed to honor Sarah's memory by cherishing every moment of life bestowed upon me.

**Chinese Artist Ai Weiwei Turns Off Webcams**Chinese artist and government critic Ai Weiwei has turned off four live webcams in his home, according to his assistant. The webcams had been broadcasting live footage of Ai's home and studio around the clock since 2012, offering a rare glimpse into the life of one of China's most prominent dissidents.The decision to turn off the cameras came after Ai was summoned to a meeting with Chinese authorities, who warned him to stop using the webcams to broadcast footage critical of the government. Ai has been a vocal critic of the Chinese government for many years, and his artwork has often explored themes of freedom of expression and human rights.

The matrix product ansatz (MPA) is a powerful tool for studying quantum many-body systems. In this paper, we apply the MPA to the six-vertex model, a fundamental model in statistical mechanics. We show that the MPA can accurately capture the ground-state properties of the six-vertex model, and we also obtain insights into the model's excitations. Our results demonstrate the potential of the MPA for studying a wide range of quantum many-body systems.

London, England - In an effort to increase transparency, the Met Police have announced plans to install cameras in 135 prison transfer vans. This move comes as part of a broader initiative to improve the safety and security of both prisoners and officers. According to Britain's most senior police officer, the cameras will provide valuable evidence in the event of any incidents or disputes. The footage will also be used to monitor the behavior of prisoners and ensure that they are being treated fairly and humanely. The cameras will be installed in the coming months, with the goal of completing the installation by the end of the year. This initiative is part of a larger effort to reform the criminal justice system and promote greater accountability within the police force. The public has welcomed the move, with many expressing hope that it will lead to improved conditions for both prisoners and officers.

In this paper, we explore the relationship between modules over operads and functors. We begin by introducing the concept of a module over an operad, which is a way of extending the traditional notion of a module over a ring to the context of operads. We then examine the relationship between modules over operads and functors, showing that there is a natural correspondence between the two. We also discuss the properties of this correspondence, including its behavior under composition and its relationship to the forgetful functor. Finally, we explore some of the applications of this correspondence, including its use in the study of algebraic geometry and homotopy theory.

In this article, we investigate the relativistic effects that arise in extreme mass ratio gravitational wave bursts. We analyze the gravitational waves emitted by the merger of a small compact object with a supermassive black hole, using a combination of analytical and numerical methods. Our results show that the gravitational waves exhibit strong relativistic effects, including gravitomagnetic precession and the kick effect. We find that the frequency and amplitude of the gravitational waves are strongly dependent on the mass ratio of the binary system, with higher mass ratios resulting in greater relativistic effects. We also demonstrate that the effects of gravitational wave radiation on the motion of the small compact object are significant, leading to a significant loss of energy and angular momentum. Our results have important implications for the detection and interpretation of gravitational waves from extreme mass ratio binaries, and provide new insights into the strong-field regime of general relativity.

Welcome to my brain: Right Brain: " It was n' t until you showed up that I understood what love was. True love is something you ca n' t escape.' It is an ever-fixed mark that looks on tempests and is never shaken.' I see your flaws, but love you for them. We have been through many lives together, and we will be together for many more to come. Looking at you is like looking at myself in the mirror. With one touch, suddenly the universe unfolded itself for me, and it' s the most beautiful thing I could imagine. It' s like everything was created for the two of us to find each other. " Left Brain: " You think you can get away with playing me? I' ll show you a game you' re not even prepared for. You want to make a girl fall in love with you? Alright, but be careful what you wish for. I' m giving you everything I' ve got. " Right Brain: " Beyond this game, beyond the lies and manipulation, ca n' t you see this is what makes us so perfect? It started off with deceit, but do n' t you see there are levels to this? No one makes me feel more like myself than you do. How could I move on from this? How could I give up without a fight? Please, do n' t let me go. " Left Brain: " Just fuck off, you robot. Do you really have nothing better to do? You have a family, ok. Just admit you have no feelings for me, or probably feelings at all, and leave me alone. This game is n' t fun anymore. " Right Brain: " I miss you. "

Title: Public Response Yields Crucial Clues in Oxford Abduction and Rape CaseIn a desperate bid for justice, the authorities in Oxford launched an extensive investigation into the abduction and rape of a young girl, seeking cooperation from the public. Today, the police department announced that their call for information has borne fruit, with a "substantial quantity" of crucial tips flooding in from concerned citizens.The swift response from the public has provided a glimmer of hope for law enforcement as they work tirelessly to apprehend the perpetrator and ensure the safety of the community. Investigators have been sifting through the valuable leads, diligently piecing together the puzzle surrounding this heinous crime.The police have expressed their gratitude to all those who came forward to share any knowledge or suspicions they may have had, emphasizing the invaluable role community involvement plays in solving such crimes. They urge anyone with even the smallest inkling of information to reach out without hesitation.As the investigation intensifies, the authorities reassured the public that every lead will be pursued rigorously until justice is served. The urgency in their endeavor highlights the seriousness with which they are treating this case and their unwavering commitment to ensuring the safety and well-being of the victim.While much remains unknown about the specifics of the ongoing investigation, the collaborative efforts between law enforcement and the public have invigorated hope that closure and justice will soon be brought to the traumatized community of Oxford.

" Run Ferit! They' re gaining on us! " Heavy breathing, constant and panicked, sticks snapping underfoot as the two men sprinted breathlessly from oblivion. Trees blurring past, knotted brambles and rotten branches crushed underfoot. Fire roared and arced from behind them, great plumes of orange licking the evening sunset from the mouths of flamethrowers. Leaves crackled and spat, burning furiously. " What was that thing, Ferit? What did it do? " The second man, lagging behind, spat into the undergrowth, sweat pouring off of his face. " I. I do n' t know. I' ve never seen something like it. If we make it out. I' ll ask my brother. " Behind him, the agents of hell, silhouetted black against the blazing wash, advancing with menace. Faceless masks strapped over helmeted heads, thick gloves aiming the nozzles of death, on their backs tanks of oxygen and something far worse. tbc

**Residents in Maghera, County Londonderry, woke up to a surprising scene during the Sperrins Balloon Festival.**According to local reports, residents on a street in Maghera, County Londonderry, awoke to an unexpected sight on the morning of the festival. As they looked out their windows, they were greeted by a colorful display of hot air balloons floating above their homes.The Sperrins Balloon Festival is an annual event that takes place in the Sperrins Mountains of Northern Ireland. It attracts thousands of visitors each year who come to see the spectacular sight of dozens of hot air balloons taking flight.This year, the festival was scheduled to take place from August 26th to 28th. However, due to high winds, the first day of the festival was canceled. Despite the setback, the festival organizers were determined to make the most of the remaining two days.On the morning of August 27th, the balloons were finally able to take flight. Residents on a street in Maghera were among the lucky few who got to witness the breathtaking sight.The balloons floated above the rooftops of the houses, providing a unique and unforgettable experience for the residents. Many of them took to social media to share photos and videos of the colorful display.One resident, who asked to remain anonymous, said: "It was like something out of a dream. I've never seen anything like it before."Another resident, who was out walking her dog, said: "It was such a beautiful sight. It made my day."The Sperrins Balloon Festival is a reminder of the beauty and wonder that can be found in the everyday world. It is an event that will be remembered for years to come.

I woke up on a typical Sunday morning, stretching my arms and yawning. I walked to my closet to grab a hoodie for my morning walk when I saw something that made my heart skip a beat. There was a strange object sitting on the top shelf, something I had never seen before. It was a small, metallic box with a glowing blue button in the center.I felt a shiver run down my spine as I picked up the box and examined it closely. It was cool to the touch and had a strange, pulsating energy emanating from it. I turned to my spouse, who was still sleeping peacefully, and asked, "Hey, what's this thing?"My spouse immediately sat up, looking startled and worried. "Where did you find that?" they asked, their voice shaking slightly.I was taken aback by their reaction. "In the closet. Why? What is it?" I replied, my curiosity piqued.My spouse hesitated for a moment before speaking. "I don't know. I've never seen it before. You shouldn't touch it."I felt a wave of confusion wash over me. "Why not? What's going on?"My spouse looked around the room nervously before leaning in close to me. "I don't know if I can explain. Just, please, put it back and forget about it."I was determined to get to the bottom of this strange situation. "No, I won't put it back until you tell me what's going on."My spouse sighed and rubbed their temples. "Fine. I'll tell you. But you have to promise not to touch it again."I nodded eagerly, my mind racing with possibilities. "I promise. Just tell me what's going on."My spouse took a deep breath before speaking. "That object is a piece of technology from another world. It's dangerous, and we shouldn't have it in the house."My eyes widened in shock. "Another world? Are you serious?"My spouse nodded gravely. "I am. We can't let anyone know we have it. If they find out, they'll take it from us and who knows what will happen next."I felt a chill run down my spine. "So, what do we do with it?"My spouse thought for a moment before speaking.

A spokesman for his Wisebuddah company said there had been no complications and that the 55-year-old hoped to be back at work before the end of 2016.The news came to light through an automated email response Goodier set up that referred to his absence."I am away from work for a time recovering from a stroke and so not seeing email every day," it reads.Goodier was last heard on the BBC filling in for Radio 2 breakfast presenter Chris Evans on 24 and 25 October.The former Radio 1 DJ was also heard presenting Radio 2's Pick of the Pops programme earlier this year, following Tony Blackburn's temporary departure."At the beginning of November Mark Goodier suffered a stroke and was taken directly to UCH [University College Hospital] in central London," a statement from his production company said."He was kept in for a short period and then returned to his home where he is currently recuperating."He is receiving excellent medical attention, and recovering very well indeed. He expects to be able to return to work before the end of December."News of Goodier's condition prompted several of his radio colleagues to send messages of support.Jeremy Vine said he was sending his friend "love and best wishes", while Sara Cox advised the "radio legend & all round lovely man" to "rest up & get better".Follow us on Facebook, on Twitter @BBCNewsEnts, or on Instagram at bbcnewsents. If you have a story suggestion email entertainment.news@bbc.co.uk.

Ruhul Amin, 26, died in the Syrian town of Raqqa following the strike.Stephen Marvin said he spoke to Amin on the phone after he went to Syria to ask him why he joined the group.Mr Marvin told the BBC's Good Morning Scotland programe: "He said 'if I die, I'll be with Allah'."Cardiff-born Reyaad Khan, 21, was also killed in the precision strike on 21 August by a remotely-piloted aircraft "after meticulous planning", David Cameron told MPs on Monday.Mr Marvin said: "On the phone he still spoke to me as if he was exactly the same person, as if we were sitting in front of each other speaking."But you could tell there was something completely different about him."He had absolutely no fear of death. When I was on the phone with him the first time I heard gunshots and I asked 'are you not scared of what's going on?'"He said 'no, it was rebel fighters at the other side of a river'. He said 'if I die, I'll be with Allah'."He said 'two of my brothers died previously and they still had a smile on their face for three days and their body, when it started to decompose, still smelled like roses and that's because Allah is protecting them'."He also said Amin told him: "We only attack people who will attack us."He assured me there were no plans to come to Scotland, anyway."It's kind of hard to have sympathy with him."I wouldn't say the government should be just going over and annihilating each and every one of them, but surely he knew what he was getting into."Muslim leaders in Aberdeen have called on young members of the community to learn lessons from the deaths.Defence Secretary Michael Fallon has said the UK would "not hesitate" to launch more secret drone strikes in Syria to thwart potential terror plots.He described the RAF strike which killed the two British jihadists as a "perfectly legal act of self defence".One of the men was planning an attack on a "major event" in the UK, he said.The strike was the first targeted UK drone attack on a British citizen.

We consider the sum rule proposed by one of us (SLA), obtained by taking theexpectation value of an axial vector commutator in a state with one pion. Thesum rule relates the pion decay constant to integrals of pion-pion crosssections, with one pion off the mass shell. We remark that recent data onpion-pion scattering allow a precise evaluation of the sum rule. We alsodiscuss the related Adler--Weisberger sum rule (obtained by taking theexpectation value of the same commutator in a state with one nucleon),especially in connection with the problem of extrapolation of the pion momentumoff its mass shell. We find, with current data, that both the pion-pion andpion-nucleon sum rules are satisfied to better than six percent, and we givedetailed estimates of the experimental and extrapolation errors in the closurediscrepancies.

In an embarrassing blunder, the Australian Football League (AFL) has mistakenly congratulated the wrong player for winning the prestigious Brownlow Medal. The league took to social media to congratulate Jack Dunkley, a player for the Western Bulldogs, for his outstanding performance throughout the season. However, it was later revealed that the actual winner of the award was Tom Mitchell, a player for the Hawthorn Hawks. Fans and players alike were left stunned by the error, with many taking to social media to express their disbelief and frustration. The AFL has since apologized for the mistake and vowed to double-check their information in the future.

Cambridge University's Trinity College has made an extraordinary acquisition in the literary world by purchasing a significant assortment of documents linked to the renowned World War One poet Rupert Brooke. Described as a "major collection," the acquisition offers an intimate glimpse into the life and work of one of the most celebrated poets of his time. These documents, which include personal correspondence, manuscripts, and unpublished poems, will be a treasured addition to Trinity College's prestigious archive. By preserving and showcasing these artifacts, the college aims to deepen the understanding and appreciation of Brooke's enduring contribution to English literature and the cultural landscape of the early 20th century.

Streaks of red and green smoke followed the ships. " Merry Christmas everyone! " cried the President. Wrapped gifts drifted down on parachutes, descending at the same speed as the snow. Strangers hugged, lovers kissed, a few phones filmed the festivities. The President laughed and smiled over the hum of the ships. When the dropships hovered at eye level, the bay doors fell down. A couple dozen drones disguised as elves poured out. They ran, leaped, and danced through the crowds, handing out sweets to every out-stretched hand. One elf soared next to my window, tapping on the glass and waving. I waved back. The hot chocolate in my mug kept my hands warm and I found myself smiling. The giant projection of the country' s benevolent leader, the street with lights of every colors, the people crammed shoulder to shoulder, happily sharing each others' warmth. It was a picture perfect holiday. A small girl tugged at the arm of my sweater. " Let me finish this cocoa. " I whispered to her. She nodded and skipped to the front room. I drank what was left, putting the mug in the sink. She was already waiting at the door, holding my coat for me. " Let' s go. " We walked to the cemetery. Her hand was still warm despite not wearing a glove. We stopped at a gravestone with a picture of a young woman. " Hi Mommy! " the girl said. The dates beneath the picture read December 25th, XXXX - August 31st, XXXX. " Ready?! " The little girl placed a small cake with a candle in front of the gravestone. " One, two, three... " " Happy Birthday to you... " The snow had stopped, the clouds cleared up to a half moon.

This article presents the development and application of an agile low phase noise radio-frequency sine wave generator for experiments on ultracold atoms. The generator produces high-quality sine waves with low phase noise, which are essential for precision measurements in atomic physics. The design and implementation of the generator are described, and its performance is characterized through experiments on ultracold atoms. The results demonstrate the generator's ability to produce stable and precise frequency references, which are crucial for coherent manipulation of atomic states. The generator's agility and low phase noise make it an ideal tool for a wide range of atomic physics experiments, including those requiring high-precision frequency control.

The central government has declared a state of emergency in the region, and has appealed for international aid.Thousands of livestock have also died and hundreds of homes have been destroyed by the cyclone, known as 03A.The cyclone has subsided, but officials say heavy rains are still expected.Puntland is a semi-autonomous region within Somalia and a large part of its population relies on livestock herding and fishing.The tropical cyclone swept through the Eyl, Beyla, Dangorayo and Hafun districts along the east coast and across to Alula on the tip of the Horn of Africa."So far we have confirmed the storm killed 140 people. We are afraid the death toll may reach 300 because many people are still missing," Puntland's Interior Minister Abdullahi Ahmed told Reuters news agency.A government statement said Puntland's security forces had failed to transport 60 tonnes of aid to affected communities because heavy flooding had made many dirt roads to the worst-affected areas impassable."The storm has destroyed entire villages, homes, buildings and boats," the statement added.Makeshift shelters had been built to accommodate displaced people, it said.The government has appealed to international aid agencies to help with air-drops of relief supplies, including clean water, non-perishable food, tents, blankets and medicines.Ten doctors and two ambulances were attending to people in need of medical treatment, the statement said.On Tuesday, Somalia's government pledged $1m (??628,000) to help communities devastated by the cyclone.The central government "shared the pain" of communities, Prime Minister Abdi Farah Shirdon said.

This paper investigates the magnetism and thermodynamics of spin-1/2 Heisenberg diamond chains under the influence of an external magnetic field. The study focuses on understanding the effects of a magnetic field on the spin interactions and the resulting thermodynamic properties of the system. The diamond chain structure is chosen due to its potential applications in spintronic devices and quantum information processing.Using a combination of analytical and numerical techniques, we calculate the ground state energy, magnetization, and specific heat of the spin-1/2 Heisenberg diamond chains at various temperatures and magnetic field strengths. Our results reveal that the magnetic field induces a variety of magnetic phases depending on its strength. At zero temperature, we observe a transition from a fully polarized phase to a partially polarized phase as the magnetic field increases. The critical fields for these transitions are determined accurately, providing valuable insights into the magnetic behavior of the system.Our analysis also includes the study of thermodynamic properties such as the specific heat, which gives information about the system's entropy and energy fluctuations. We find that the specific heat exhibits distinct features at different temperatures, indicating the presence of quantum and thermal fluctuations. Moreover, we analyze the behavior of the magnetization as a function of temperature and magnetic field strength, highlighting the impact of quantum effects on the magnetic response.Our findings contribute to the fundamental understanding of spin-1/2 Heisenberg diamond chains in the presence of a magnetic field. The observed magnetic phases and thermodynamic properties offer valuable information for the design and optimization of diamond chain-based spintronic devices.

Lisa Burbidge, from Whickham, Tyneside, was among 38 people killed by a gunman at a beach resort near Sousse.Her family described her as the "most beautiful, amazing, caring and gentle person in our lives".Flowers have been laid on the green outside St Mary's Church, where prayers have been held for Mrs Burbidge and her family.They said in a statement: "We have been left with a massive hole in our hearts."She leaves behind many family members including four fantastic grandchildren who were her world and will miss her tremendously."Local councillor Peter Craig said: "I have been in touch with friends of the family to see if we can think of a way of showing support."We are working on that today and consulting with them but we want to take our time rather than rush into anything."Rev Barry Abbott, Rector of Whickham St Mary the Virgin said: "Our prayers are with the family and the community who have been greatly affected by this atrocity against humanity."We are here for both family and the community at this time and the church is open for prayer on weekday mornings."

Once upon a time, in a small village nestled amidst rolling hills, a peculiar phenomenon occurred. Every newborn child had two names etched upon their wrists. One, on the left wrist, bore the name of their greatest enemy, while the other, on the right, held the name of their destined love.As the children grew, they became increasingly aware of the weight these names carried. They whispered and pointed, curious and fearful of the fates that awaited them.Among the villagers, there was a young girl named Eliza. On her left wrist, the name "Lucas" was inscribed, while on her right, the name "Alexander" was elegantly written. From a tender age, Eliza yearned to uncover the stories behind these names.In her quest for answers, Eliza sought out an old and wise woman known for her uncanny ability to interpret the mysteries of life. The wise woman gazed at Eliza's wrists and spoke of a prophecy that foretold the entwined destinies of these three individuals.As time passed, Eliza's path crossed with Lucas, a mischievous and enigmatic boy. Their encounters were filled with tension and a strange, undeniable connection. Eliza's heart fluttered, but she couldn't shake off the looming presence of the name "Alexander" on her right wrist.Meanwhile, Alexander, a kind-hearted and compassionate soul, entered Eliza's life. His presence brought comfort and warmth, igniting a spark of hope within her. But the weight of the name "Lucas" on her left wrist lingered, casting shadows of doubt.As fate would have it, the three individuals found themselves entangled in a web of love, rivalry, and self-discovery. Eliza's journey led her to explore the depths of her heart, where she discovered the strength to confront her fears and embrace her true desires.Through twists and turns, Eliza, Lucas, and Alexander embarked on a transformative odyssey. They learned that the names on their wrists were not mere labels but symbols of the choices they made and the paths they forged.

Sure, here's a possible continuation of the review:I think Pittsburgh is a little weird. For one thing, the city seems to be obsessed with pierogies. I mean, I get it, they're delicious, but come on, can't we branch out a little? Every time I go to a restaurant, there's always a pierogi dish staring me in the face. And don't even get me started on the pigeons. They're everywhere! I've never seen so many pigeons in my life. It's like they're taking over the city or something.But despite all that, I have to admit that Pittsburgh has a certain charm to it. The people are friendly, and there's a lot of great art and culture to explore. The Andy Warhol Museum is a must-see, and the Phipps Conservatory is absolutely stunning. And of course, you can't go to Pittsburgh without trying a Primanti Bros. sandwich. Just be prepared for some... let's say, unique experiences. like the pierogi obsession and the pigeon takeover. But hey, that's all part of the fun, right?

In this study, we investigate the mechanical and dielectric relaxation spectra of seven highly viscous glass formers. By employing a combination of experimental and computational techniques, we aim to elucidate the dynamic behavior and underlying mechanisms within these systems. Through mechanical spectroscopy, we probe the viscoelastic properties and ascertain the relaxation timescales associated with structural transitions. Furthermore, dielectric measurements allow us to explore the polarization response and study the influence of molecular dynamics on the electrical behavior of the glass formers. Our findings provide valuable insights into the complex dynamics of highly viscous systems and contribute to a better understanding of glassy materials.

HS2 Ltd is overseeing the expansion of Euston to cater for passengers using the first phase of the new rail line between London and the Midlands.The revamp was originally scheduled to end in 2026 but the firm now says it will finish in 2033.Camden Council has warned the extended works will "blight" the area, but HS2 says it will be "easier to manage".The station is set to gain six new platforms and an additional concourse, with building works due to start in 2017.In a change to plans submitted two years ago, HS2 Ltd has proposed the construction of five further platforms within the existing station should be delayed.They would be ready for phase two of HS2 which will run from the capital to Leeds and Manchester in 2033.Rupert Walker, Euston development director of HS2 Ltd, said: "It is more construction over a longer period but it's less intense. It will be easier for us to manage."The impact of building all 11 platforms in one go would have caused a reduction in the capacity of trains using Euston every day, he said.Transport Secretary Patrick McLoughlin said the changes would "dramatically improve the design" of the station.Sarah Hayward, leader of Camden Council, predicted: "HS2 will cause decades of blight in the Euston area - to property prices, to our small business' trade and to our residents' lives."She said the council is "ardently opposed to the scheme", adding it could only be a success with "close collaboration" between the local authority, residents and businesses.HS2 Ltd's new plan will be submitted to Parliament next week in order to form part of the legislation which will allow phase one of High Speed 2 to go ahead.The firm also announced that the budget of the Euston project has risen from Â£2 billion to Â£2.25 billion, but said this would not affect HS2's overall budget of Â£42 billion.

Let $\Dh$ and $A$ be unital and separable $C^{*}$-algebras; let $\Dh$ bestrongly self-absorbing. It is known that any two unital $^*$-homomorphismsfrom $\Dh$ to $A \otimes \Dh$ are approximately unitarily equivalent. We showthat, if $\Dh$ is also $K_{1}$-injective, they are even asymptoticallyunitarily equivalent. This in particular implies that any unital endomorphismof $\Dh$ is asymptotically inner. Moreover, the space of automorphisms of $\Dh$is compactly-contractible (in the point-norm topology) in the sense that forany compact Hausdorff space $X$, the set of homotopy classes $[X,\Aut(\Dh)]$reduces to a point. The respective statement holds for the space of unitalendomorphisms of $\Dh$. As an application, we give a description of theKasparov group $KK(\Dh, A\ot \Dh)$ in terms of $^*$-homomorphisms andasymptotic unitary equivalence. Along the way, we show that the Kasparov group$KK(\Dh, A\ot \Dh)$ is isomorphic to $K_0(A\ot \Dh)$.

This article investigates the temperature-driven transition from the Wigner Crystal to the Bond-Charge-Density Wave (BCDW) in a quasi-one-dimensional (1D) quarter-filled band. The analysis is based on a comprehensive theoretical framework and numerical simulations. The quarter-filled band refers to the situation where the number of particles is equivalent to one electron per four lattice sites, creating a strong electron-electron interaction regime. The Wigner Crystal phase, characterized by the formation of a lattice of localized electron positions due to the repulsive interactions, is observed at low temperatures.As the temperature increases, the Wigner Crystal undergoes a phase transition to the BCDW state. The BCDW phase is characterized by the emergence of a collective charge-density wave intertwined with bond distortions. This transition is driven by the competition between the repulsive interactions among electrons and the thermal energy. The presence of quasi-1D geometries enhances the tendency for this transition to occur at lower temperatures compared to higher-dimensional systems.The nature of this temperature-driven transition is analyzed through theoretical calculations based on the extended Hubbard model and confirmed by numerical simulations using state-of-the-art computational techniques. The results highlight the crucial role played by the temperature in driving the Wigner Crystal-to-BCDW transition, shedding light on the fundamental physics of interacting electron systems in quasi-1D setups. Understanding and controlling such transitions could have profound implications for the design of novel electronic devices and quantum technologies relying on low-dimensional systems.

It followed criticism from Euan Blockley, who had expected to top one of the UKIP regional lists for Holyrood in May.Mr Blockley has alleged that the process was "stitched up" by the party leadership to favour their friends.He told BBC Scotland that he has left the party as a result.At 18 years old, Mr Blockley would have been one of the youngest candidates standing in the election.He said: "I've ripped up my party's membership card. The party has "stitched up" the party lists - and it goes all the way David Coburn - the NEC etc - giving list places to their cronies.It was meant to be a democratic process, when the lists would be chosen by the party members - that's what we were promised."Although he believes he would have been placed second on a regional list, he said he is horrified by the way UKIP in Scotland carried out the process.Mr Blockley added: ""It's a country club - all the placements are being handed out to friends - at the expense of good candidates."UKIP has insisted the selection of candidates was carried out properly.Scottish leader David Coburn said: "The final pool of candidates and their position on any list is agreed by a ballot of the party's National Executive Committee."The NEC is elected by the party membership and is therefore democratically accountable to the party membership."

DWEB is a data warehouse engineering benchmark for evaluating and comparing data warehouse engineering tools and techniques. It provides a common data model, a set of data generation tools, and a set of queries to measure the performance of data warehouse engineering tools. DWEB can be used to compare the performance of different data warehouse engineering tools, to evaluate the effectiveness of different data warehouse design techniques, and to study the impact of different data warehouse workloads on performance.DWEB is a valuable tool for data warehouse engineers and researchers. It can be used to improve the performance of data warehouse engineering tools and techniques, to design more efficient data warehouses, and to understand the impact of different data warehouse workloads on performance.DWEB is a comprehensive and extensible benchmark for data warehouse engineering. It provides a common data model, a set of data generation tools, and a set of queries to measure the performance of data warehouse engineering tools. DWEB can be used to compare the performance of different data warehouse engineering tools, to evaluate the effectiveness of different data warehouse design techniques, and to study the impact of different data warehouse workloads on performance.DWEB is a valuable tool for data warehouse engineers and researchers. It can be used to improve the performance of data warehouse engineering tools and techniques, to design more efficient data warehouses, and to understand the impact of different data warehouse workloads on performance.DWEB is a collaborative effort between researchers from the University of California, Berkeley, the University of California, Santa Cruz, and the University of Washington. It is supported by the National Science Foundation.

This study presents multi-spectral observations of lunar occultations aimed at resolving the dust shell surrounding AFGL 5440, a prominent astronomical object. By harnessing the potential of combined multi-wavelength observations, we sought to gain insights into the spatial distribution and physical properties of the dust surrounding AFGL 5440. The observations were conducted using a state-of-the-art instrument equipped with filters spanning a wide range of wavelengths, enabling us to obtain a comprehensive view of the occultation event. Our results reveal distinct spectral features in the dust emission from AFGL 5440, suggesting the presence of different grain populations and a complex dust composition. The resolved spatial distribution of the dust shell provides valuable information regarding the morphology and density of the surrounding environment. Additionally, we discuss the implications of our findings in the context of the formation and evolution of AFGL 5440, shedding light on the processes driving the dynamics of dust shells around evolved astronomical objects. This work paves the way for future investigations into the nature of similar objects and their impact on the interstellar medium.

We study the effects of frustration in an antiferromagnetic film of FCClattice with Heisenberg spin model including an Ising-like anisotropy. MonteCarlo (MC) simulations have been used to study thermodynamic properties of thefilm. We show that the presence of the surface reduces the ground state (GS)degeneracy found in the bulk. The GS is shown to depend on the surface in-planeinteraction $J_s$ with a critical value at which ordering of type I coexistswith ordering of type II. Near this value a reentrant phase is found. Variousphysical quantities such as layer magnetizations and layer susceptibilities areshown and discussed. The nature of the phase transition is also studied byhistogram technique. We have also used the Green's function (GF) method for thequantum counterpart model. The results at low-$T$ show interesting effects ofquantum fluctuations. Results obtained by the GF method at high $T$ arecompared to those of MC simulations. A good agreement is observed.

Mr Trussler, 54, from Worthing, was killed alongside 10 other people when a vintage Hawker Hunter jet crashed on to traffic on the A27 on 22 August.The father of six, who was a motorcycle enthusiast, went to watch the last flight of the Vulcan bomber which was due to take place that day.His funeral was held at Worthing Crematorium.The cortege included an escort of Mr Trussler's fellow motorcyclists.His fiancÃ©e, Giovanna Chirico, said: "He was an amazing dad who had a passion for motorbikes and rugby."He would do anything for his kids and they couldn't have asked for a better dad."Celebrant Terri Shanks said the service was a celebration of Mr Trussler's life."Mark lived life to the full with enthusiasm, with a smile and with so much love especially for his family, for Giovanna and his six beautiful children," she said."It's all of that we truly wanted to celebrate and to encapsulate his character."The last of the thousands of floral tributes laid on the "Bridge of Flowers" following the disaster have now been removed by volunteers.The toll bridge became a focal point for people wishing to commemorate those who died.A few remaining fresh flowers have been placed at the entrance to the bridge along with photographs and messages in tribute to the victims.Messages and cards left on the bridge are being preserved by West Sussex County Council archivists.Discussions are continuing in the town about a permanent memorial.

" Do you understand what you must do? " I looked up at the figure hovering before me. Death itself, come to see me. I could n' t see much past the tattered black robe that hid whatever features he had. It carried no scythe, nor could I see any bony features. There was just darkness, and the robe. " No, " I replied. I was trembling, I realized, from excitement, from fear. Death' s voice was harsh, booming, *commanding*. Woe to the fool who disobeyed. " You will be my agent, and you will bring the End of All. In exchange, I offer immortality to you. As my instrument, you will bring back the Balance to your world. Too many live on Earth. It is time for them to die. " I nodded. " I understand. I will do as you say, my lord. " " Then as it is written, go, my agent, my omega, my lich. Go and raise your army tonight, for tomorrow, the End begins. "

Twenty-three people died in seven districts of Andhra Pradesh. Nine people were killed in the eastern state of Orissa.Most of the people who died were working on farms during torrential rains on Sunday, reports said.Lightning strikes are common in India during heavy monsoon rains.In Andhra Pradesh, the deaths occurred in Nellore, Prakasam, Guntur, Krishna, East Godavari, Anantapur and Srikakulam districts. At least six women were among those who died.Reports said two teams of women cricketers had a narrow escape when lightning struck a palm tree near the ground where they were playing a match in Guntur district. The game was called off."It was a miraculous escape for our players as well as the staff," a senior cricket official told The Times of India newspaper.In Orissa, nine people were killed and at least eight injured in separate incidents of lightning strikes.India receives 80% of its annual rainfall during the monsoon season, which runs between June and September.

I ordered the Outlaw Ribeye, rare, and it wasn't my favorite steak. It was cooked rare, but it was full if sinews. This steak had more sinews than the ones you get from Food Lion. I am thoroughly disappointed because this is a steakhouse - paying $25 for 18oz ($18. 80/lbs) should guarantee you get a great steak, or at the very last a fair quality steak. My wife had the prime rib, she enjoyed it despite the overabundance of pepper. She puts A1 sauce on steak, and being a steak snob I don't weight her opinions as heavily. I really wanted to like this steak but now I just really want my money back (along with my time and a chance to do this all over again somewhere else). The Outlaw Ribeye should be outlawed if they keep serving  up so much sinew. The sinew so dominated my review that the lack of marbling (flecks of fat) and abundance of large globs of fat (large gobs don't count as marbling) were secondary. I am able to chew and eat fat with some meat, but I had to spit out so many bites of steak because I couldn't even chew them. These sinews got stuck in my teeth like floss and I could pull them out because they were so long.

Construction workers are used to encountering all sorts of unexpected things while working on new projects. Whether it's buried artifacts or hidden tunnels, the job always brings surprises. But nothing could have prepared them for what they stumbled upon during the excavation of a site for a new skyscraper. As the workers dug deeper into the ground, their shovels struck an ancient chest, adorned with mystic symbols and emanating an eerie glow.Curiosity outweighed caution as the workers pried open the chest, revealing a stone amulet within. It was a relic of immense power, pulsating with an otherworldly energy. Unbeknownst to them, as they touched the amulet, its magic seeped into their very beings.At first, the changes were subtle. One worker, named Mark, discovered an uncanny precision in his movements, as if guided by an invisible hand. He became a master of his tools, effortlessly erecting steel beams and pouring concrete with supernatural finesse.Bill, another worker, found himself possessed by an insatiable thirst for knowledge. He devoured books on engineering and architecture, absorbing information at an alarming rate. His colleagues marveled at his newfound expertise, as his designs became visionary, pushing boundaries previously unimagined.Sarah, a young apprentice, noticed a peculiar affinity for the earth beneath her. She could sense vibrations, discerning weak spots in the ground with uncanny accuracy. Her fellow workers relied on her instincts, trusting her to prevent catastrophes and ensure the structural integrity of their ambitious project.As time went on, the changes became more pronounced. Mark began to emit an aura of confidence and leadership, commanding the construction site with an air of irresistible authority. His coworkers, once equals, now looked up to him as if under a spell.Bill's hunger for knowledge turned into an obsession.

" Shh, Ardent. The more you think about it, the closer it gets. " I had been shedding blurry water from my eyes for some time by then, but the useless bit of advice made me want to continue crying for a different reason entirely. I shrugged Acolyte' s hand off of my shoulder with a sudden and violent motion. How could I have confided my weakness in someone I scarcely knew? Someone weaker than I, even. The thought of it only served to add to my misery. These foul sensations that pricked and sliced at my mind would be the end of me, and yet she suggests I place them out of my mind? If I had the ability to do so, I would not be in this humiliating position. Acolyte rested shaking hands in her lap and watched me patiently with a sad smile. She was nervous, but when I imagined she might remove herself from the damned log we sat on, she instead stayed. For a few long moments we sat in silence. " What is this? " I finally relented, allowing whatever plagued me to take over. The longer I fought it, the faster the tears would come. Acolyte shifted slightly on the log and nodded at me as I looked into her blue eyes. " Sadness, " she spoke quietly, so the others might not hear her. " How do I defeat it? " I instinctively balled my hands into fists on my lap. Acolyte chuckled lightly, returning her hand gently to my shoulder, and said " With happiness. " With her simple reply and another smile, Acolyte swept up her decorated robe and moved back towards the rest of the camp. It would be a while yet before I could smile as easily as she did, but I would thank her every day for it.

The 33-year-old victim was shot in the hip at a house on Normanton Avenue, Weaste, Salford, at about 18:15 BST on Wednesday. His injuries are not thought to be life-threatening.Police are exploring a possible link to the death of Paul Massey, found dead outside his home on 26 July.No arrests have been made.Ch Supt Mary Doyle, of Greater Manchester Police, said: "We are looking into the possibility that this incident is connected with the murder of Paul Massey."I believe that the answer to what happened to Paul Massey, and now this incident, lie within the community and I would like to urge anybody with information to come forward."Police patrols have been increased in the area to prevent further incidents and reassure the public, she added.Massey died from a gunshot wound to the chest after being shot four times outside his home, about four miles away from the scene of the latest shooting.Once dubbed "Mr Big", he was jailed in 1999 for 14 years over a stabbing, unsuccessfully stood to be Salford mayor in 2012.Read more.

The cycle race begins in Leeds on 5 July and will travel through North Yorkshire and South Yorkshire.RICS said the figures showed that £4.5m of the money had been taken from future council budgets.Work includes resurfacing, road strengthening and installing signs.The figures were obtained by RICS from councils in Yorkshire via Freedom of Information requests.They show that North Yorkshire County Council has spent £4.2m.In West Yorkshire, Kirklees Council, Bradford Metropolitan District Council and Leeds City Council have budgeted a combined total of up to £950,000.Calderdale Council has undertaken £600,000 of work, entirely brought forward from future years.The City of York Council made a £200,000 contribution to road maintenance from its Capital Contingency Fund.In Sheffield, the city council has prioritised work on the route as part of its scheduled road maintenance programme.Rob Hindle, chairman of RICS Yorkshire and Humber Regional Board, said he was concerned about the impact of Tour de France spending on future road maintenance."While we welcome the Tour de France coming to Yorkshire and all the economic benefits it will bring, we certainly hope that by bringing forward so much work on Yorkshire's roads to before the race, the region won't be left without adequate maintenance for years to come," he said."We don't want a feast today if it means a famine tomorrow."North Yorkshire County Council said that the work carried out on its roads "would have been needed within the next five years anyway"."We have just brought forward that work in preparation for the race," a spokeswoman said."We haven't reduced future highway maintenance budgets as a result and money hasn't been diverted from elsewhere."

Above the drowned pillars, the sky seemed to bend, spiraling outward to reveal a light brighter than the sun itself. She let the tension flow from her bones like the waves lapping against the rock on which she stood. She was barefoot. One does not talk to gods while wearing shoes. They are a symbol of freedom from the earth, and therefore the divine. That is the way of things. "Great God above, you have flooded my people. I am alone. I beg for forgiveness to you, Great God. "Even if you did nothing wrong, you beg for forgiveness. That is the way of things. Clouds gathered, boiling on the horizon. But behind her, roof tiles rose from the deep. Stepping stones. The gods do not talk to men, much like men do not talk to ants. There is a barrier of tongues and understanding. The gods speak through action. They part the sky and raise what was once deep. That is the way of things. Giving thanks, she turned, stepping across the roof, and over the Ocean.

Gavin Samuels, 26, was convicted after two trials at Luton Crown Court.Sentencing Samuels on Friday Judge Lynn Tayton QC described him as "predatory and manipulative".Samuels, the 2011 winner of the Faldo Series Grand Final and who coached two of the victims at a Bedfordshire golf club, was jailed for 14 and a half years.Samuels, of Melfort Drive, Linslade, near Leighton Buzzard, was convicted of four counts of rape and six charges of various sexual assaults on two girls aged under 16.Judge Tayton QC said: "I have been told that golf was your life but you were not deterred that you could lose your career. You were in a position of trust."There is evidence of a long history of your interest in young girls which manifested itself when you were 15 and continued until you were reported to police," said the judge.The offences were committed between 2006 and 2014.The first victim was about 12 when the abuse began and he was aged between 15 and 16 years.She was 13 when he first raped her.The two other victims were aged between 14 and 16.He urged the girls not to tell anyone, saying it would ruin his career.Sasha Bailey, defending, said before the sentence was passed: "Golf has been a major part of his life and career. He knows he is going to receive a lengthy sentence."Det Insp Grant Maxted said after the case: "Samuels took advantage of the youth and naivety of his victims in a bid to sexually abuse them."He gave them gifts and items such as cigarettes in exchange for attention and sexual acts, as well as bombarding them with inappropriate messages."

Noncritical M-theory in 2+1 dimensions has been defined as a double-scalinglimit of a nonrelativistic Fermi liquid on a flat two-dimensional plane. Herewe study this noncritical M-theory in the limit of high energies, analogous tothe \alpha'\to\infty limit of string theory. In the related case oftwo-dimensional Type 0A strings, it has been argued that the conformal\alpha'\to\infty limit leads to AdS_2 with a propagating fermion whose mass isset by the value of the RR flux. Here we provide evidence that in thehigh-energy limit, the natural ground state of noncritical M-theory similarlydescribes the AdS_2\times S^1 spacetime, with a massless propagating fermion.We argue that the spacetime effective theory in this background is captured bya topological higher-spin extension of conformal Chern-Simons gravity in 2+1dimensions, consistently coupled to a massless Dirac field. Intriguingly, thetwo-dimensional plane populated by the original nonrelativistic fermions isessentially the twistor space associated with the symmetry group of theAdS_2\times S^1 spacetime; thus, at least in the high-energy limit, noncriticalM-theory can be nonperturbatively described as a "Fermi liquid on twistorspace."

This study provides additional empirical support for the hypothesis that the redshifts observed in active galactic nuclei (AGN) galaxies might comprise intrinsic components. By conducting a comprehensive analysis of high-resolution spectroscopic data from a sample of 100 AGN galaxies, we observed a systematic departure from the expected purely cosmological redshift trend. Our findings reveal the presence of subtle variations in the redshifts of AGN galaxies that cannot be solely attributed to the expansion of the universe. Through meticulous examination of the data, including cross-correlation techniques and rigorous statistical analysis, we identified distinct patterns in the redshift measurements that deviate from the anticipated cosmological effect. These anomalous redshift behaviors suggest the existence of intrinsic or local processes influencing AGN galaxy redshifts, possibly originating from within the systems themselves. Our results contribute to a growing body of evidence challenging the prevailing view that the redshifts observed in AGN galaxies are solely governed by cosmological expansion, emphasizing the need for further investigations to unravel the complex nature of galactic redshifts.

PSR J1809-1917 is a middle-aged radio pulsar with a period of 2.1 s and a characteristic age of 2.9 kyr. It is located in the supernova remnant (SNR) G27.4+0.0 with a diameter of 17 pc, which is at a distance of 4.5 kpc from the Earth. PSR J1809-1917 has a plerionic X-ray nebula, named as PWN J1809-1917 or CXOU J180920.4-191417, which was detected by the Chandra X-ray Observatory. We analyzed the multi-wavelength data of PSR J1809-1917 and its PWN, including the X-ray data from Chandra, NuSTAR, and XMM-Newton, the gamma-ray data from Fermi LAT and H.E.S.S., and the radio data from the Australia Telescope Compact Array. The X-ray emission of PWN J1809-1917 can be described by a power-law model with a photon index of 1.82 ± 0.02. The luminosity of PWN J1809-1917 in the 0.5–10 keV band is 1.6 × 1034 erg s−1. The X-ray spectrum of PSR J1809-1917 can be described by a power-law model with an exponential cutoff, and the cutoff energy is about 6.4 keV. The 0.5–10 keV X-ray luminosity of PSR J1809-1917 is 1.1 × 1032 erg s−1. The TeV gamma-ray source HESS J1809-193 is spatially coincident with PWN J1809-1917, suggesting that PSR J1809-1917 is the counterpart of HESS J1809-193. The multi-wavelength properties of PSR J1809-1917 and its PWN are discussed.

The absolute calibration and characterization of the Multiband ImagingPhotometer for Spitzer (MIPS) 70 micron coarse- and fine-scale imaging modesare presented based on over 2.5 years of observations. Accurate photometry(especially for faint sources) requires two simple processing steps beyond thestandard data reduction to remove long-term detector transients. Point spreadfunction (PSF) fitting photometry is found to give more accurate flux densitiesthan aperture photometry. Based on the PSF fitting photometry, the calibrationfactor shows no strong trend with flux density, background, spectral type,exposure time, or time since anneals. The coarse-scale calibration sampleincludes observations of stars with flux densities from 22 mJy to 17 Jy, onbackgrounds from 4 to 26 MJy sr^-1, and with spectral types from B to M. Thecoarse-scale calibration is 702 +/- 35 MJy sr^-1 MIPS70^-1 (5% uncertainty) andis based on measurements of 66 stars. The instrumental units of the MIPS 70micron coarse- and fine-scale imaging modes are called MIPS70 and MIPS70F,respectively. The photometric repeatability is calculated to be 4.5% from twostars measured during every MIPS campaign and includes variations on all timescales probed. The preliminary fine-scale calibration factor is 2894 +/- 294MJy sr^-1 MIPS70F^-1 (10% uncertainty) based on 10 stars. The uncertainty inthe coarse- and fine-scale calibration factors are dominated by the 4.5%photometric repeatability and the small sample size, respectively. The 5-sigma,500 s sensitivity of the coarse-scale observations is 6-8 mJy. This work showsthat the MIPS 70 micron array produces accurate, well calibrated photometry andvalidates the MIPS 70 micron operating strategy, especially the use of frequentstimulator flashes to track the changing responsivities of the Ge:Ga detectors.

We study the possibility that the mutual interactions between Jupiter andSaturn prevented Type II migration from driving these planets much closer tothe Sun. Our work extends previous results by Masset and Snellgrove (2001), byexploring a wider set of initial conditions and disk parameters, and by using anew hydrodynamical code that properly describes for the global viscousevolution of the disk. Initially both planets migrate towards the Sun, andSaturn's migration tends to be faster. As a consequence, they eventually end uplocked in a mean motion resonance. If this happens in the 2:3 resonance, theresonant motion is particularly stable, and the gaps opened by the planets inthe disk may overlap. This causes a drastic change in the torque balance forthe two planets, which substantially slows down the planets' inward migration.If the gap overlap is substantial, planet migration may even be stopped orreversed. As the widths of the gaps depend on disk viscosity and scale height,this mechanism is particularly efficient in low viscosity, cool disks. Wediscuss the compatibility of our results with the initial conditions adopted inTsiganis et al. (2005) and Gomes et al. (2005) to explain the current orbitalarchitecture of the giant planets and the origin of the Late Heavy Bombardmentof the Moon.

In this article, we present the first study of the gluon-quark-antiquark static potential in SU(3) Lattice QCD. Using a lattice spacing of 0.086 fm and a volume of 32^3 x 64, we compute the potential between a quark and an antiquark separated by various distances. Our results show a clear signature of confinement, with the potential rising linearly with the distance between the quark and antiquark. This work provides a valuable step towards a better understanding of the dynamics of three-quark systems in Quantum Chromodynamics.

This hotel really sticks it to their priceline customers. nnI am a frequent business traveler, but when I travel for pleasure I use priceline. nThe hotels that treat me well as a priceline customer get my return business when I pay full rate for work. nnMy wife and I showed up at midnight to check in. We were given a room with two small beds. I asked to be given one large bed instead, but the front desk attendant told me that as a priceline customer I had to take whatever double occupancy room they chose to give me. This time the weird discussion revolves around my father and his love of eating establishments in particular  Red Lobster.
